name: Against Malaria Foundation
mission: We help protect people from malaria We fund long-lasting insecticidal nets LLINs and work with distribution partners to ensure they are used. We track and report on net use and impact.
theoryOfChange: By providing 100% of public donations toward LLINs and ensuring their distribution and use through rigorous monitoring and data tracking, AMF reduces malaria illness and prevents deaths cost-effectively.
scoreSummary: Against Malaria Foundation (US) shows moderate impact and moderate alignment.

evaluationSources: The Life You Can Save - Top Rated 2012 to 2025
Giving What We Can - Top Rated 2012 to 2025

dimension_explanations.alignment: With an alignment score of 20/50, the charity is recognized as sadaqah-eligible for those seeking general charitable giving opportunities [6]. However, it has a low donor fit for those specifically seeking religious-centric programming or cultural alignment [1].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation demonstrates high financial health with a total revenue of $119,395,632 and a perfect program expense ratio of 100.0% [1][2]. While its cost per beneficiary is low, the impact score of 24/50 reflects its role in capacity building and the complexities of long-term malaria reduction monitoring [4][5].

baseline_narrative.summary: The Against Malaria Foundation (US) focuses on funding and distributing long-lasting insecticidal nets (LLINs) to prevent the spread of malaria [3]. In the most recent fiscal year, the organization reported a total revenue of $119,395,632 while maintaining a program expense ratio of 100.0% [1][2].

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize maximum financial efficiency and want every cent of their contribution to go directly toward purchasing life-saving supplies.
donor_motivations: Want to support humanitarian work in Muslim-majority African nations
Seek high transparency and third-party validation
giving_considerations: The organization has a very narrow focus on a single intervention (nets)
Revenue has seen a 3-year CAGR of -19.4%
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill zakat obligations or those who prefer supporting multi-sectoral community development.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The charity tracks <cite id="12">two specific outcome metrics</cite> and has <cite id="12">two external evaluation documents</cite> available.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: AMF funds LLINs and works with partners to ensure distribution, followed by tracking net usage to reduce malaria incidence.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the nets distributed are actually used and effectively reducing the malaria burden.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: Malaria remains a leading cause of death in target regions, and the charity can scale net procurement with additional funds.

strengths.detail: The charity maintains a <cite id="1">100.0% program expense ratio</cite> and spends <cite id="1">$0.00 to raise every $1</cite> in public donations.
The organization holds a <cite id="1">94/100 overall rating from Charity Navigator</cite> and a <cite id="13">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>.
AMF uses <cite id="3">post-distribution net use surveys</cite> and malaria case data to <cite id="12">track outcomes across two specific metrics</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: The Against Malaria Foundation's primary strength lies in its '100% model,' where <cite id="6">all public donations buy nets</cite>. This is made possible by a small group of private donors who fund the organization's overhead. This efficiency is paired with a <cite id="1">100/100 score in Culture and Leadership</cite> from Charity Navigator. Furthermore, the charity demonstrates strategic clarity by <cite id="12">tracking specific outcome metrics</cite> through Candid, ensuring that the <cite id="15">distribution in countries like the Democratic Republic of Congo and South Sudan</cite> results in measurable health improvements.
rich_narrative.summary: The Against Malaria Foundation (US) operates as a highly specialized humanitarian organization dedicated to the <cite id="3">funding and distribution of long-lasting insecticidal nets</cite> (LLINs). Since its <cite id="14">founding in 2004</cite>, the charity has focused on a singular, high-impact intervention to combat malaria in regions across <cite id="15">Africa, Asia, and South America</cite>. The organization is notable for its extreme financial efficiency, reporting <cite id="2">$62,286,888 in total revenue</cite> for the 2024 fiscal year while maintaining a <cite id="1">100.0% program expense ratio</cite>. This means that every dollar donated by the public is used exclusively to <cite id="6">buy nets</cite>, as private donors cover all administrative and fundraising costs. The charity has achieved <cite id="1">an overall score of 94.0/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting high standards of accountability. While the organization does not have a religious mandate, it operates extensively in <cite id="15">Muslim-majority regions such as Guinea, Chad, and Nigeria</cite>, providing critical health infrastructure to underserved populations. Its model relies on <cite id="3">rigorous post-distribution monitoring</cite> and data tracking to ensure that nets reach the intended households and remain in use over time.
